name: Cajun Shepherd's Pie Paul Prudhomme
recipeIngredient: 2 lbs White potatoes, peeled and diced
1/4 tsp White pepper, onion powder, garlic powder, cayenne pepper
recipeInstructions:
1. In a 13x9" ungreased baking pan, combine the beef and pork. Mix in the eggs and breadcrumbs by hand until mixed thoroughly. Reserve.
2. In a 1 quart saucepan, combine 3 tbsp butter with the onions, celery, green pepper, Worcestershire sauce, Tabasco, and meat seasoning mix. Saute over high heat for 5 minutes, stirring frequently and scraping the bottom well. Remove from heat and let cool.
3. Add the mixture and 1/4 cup milk to the meat mixture and mix well by hand.
4. Form into a 12x8 loaf and center in the pan. Bake in an oven preheated to 450°F for 30 minutes.
5. Remove from oven and pour off drippings, reserving 2 1/2 tbsp drippings. Set meat and drippings aside.
6. Boil the potatoes until fork tender, drain, and reserve 1 cup potato water. Place hot potatoes in a large mixing bowl.
7. Add remaining butter, 1/4 cup milk, salt, and white pepper to the potatoes. Stir with a wooden spoon until broken up, then beat with a whisk or handheld mixer. If potatoes are not creamy enough, beat in some of the reserved water.
8. In a large skillet, combine the reserved drippings with the carrots, onions, and vegetable seasoning mix. Saute over high heat for 1 1/2 minutes, stirring frequently.
9. Add zucchini and yellow squash and continue sauteing until noticeably bright in color, about 3-4 minutes, stirring occasionally.
10. Remove from heat. Mound the cooked vegetables on top of the meat loaf, keeping away from edges. Layer the mashed potatoes evenly over the vegetable layer and top edges of meat, using all of the potatoes.
11. Bake at 525°F until brown on top, about 8-10 minutes. Serve immediately.
